info.title: Create generic Spoon rule for Jakarta Validation API upgrade

parts.text: "Project @wicket-crudifier/ does not compile due to a breaking dependency update. Your goal is to generate a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule - not a one-off patch - that can be applied to ANY Maven project affected by the same breaking change. Plan: 1) Identify the compilation errors in @wicket-crudifier/ and determine which API    (class/method/constructor/signature) changed in the dependency.    The new dependency API specification is available at /workspace/jakarta.validation-api-3.0.1-api.md and the Javadoc at /workspace/javadoc-jakarta.validation-api-3.0.1/    for reference when identifying replacement types, methods, or fields. 2) Characterize the breaking change abstractly:    - What was the old API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String))    - What is the new API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String, boolean))    - What structural transformation does this require at the call site?    Do NOT assume the fix is specific to this project - any project calling the old API will need the same transformation. 3) Generate a GENERIC Spoon transformation using the template in @/workspace/spoon-base-template/:    - Match the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names.    - Use patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears.    - Parameterize by fully-qualified type names and method signatures from the dependency, NOT from the client.    - Save the transformation in /workspace/spoon-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the Spoon API documentation located in folder /workspace/spoon-api-docs/ for reference. 4) Compile the transformation and verify it has no errors. 5) Apply the transformation to @wicket-crudifier/ and ensure both compilation errors and test errors are fixed. 6) Ensure generalizability: ensure the generated rule contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers.    The rule must be applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path."

**Inspecting workspace**

I need to act on the workspace and inspect the repository. Since there's no Git repo at the root, I'll check the project directory. It’s a bit complex, so using a todo list would help. I think it might be beneficial to use multi-tool parallel for reading files. I’ll also avoid using bash for file reading and go with glob/read/grep instead. Let’s keep everything updated and send progress updates as I go!
I’m locating the project, the broken call sites, and the template/API docs so I can derive a reusable Spoon rule rather than patching this repo directly.

The validatorFactory from the legacy dependency uses javax.validation metadata, so those should also be javax. The build error is centered on the Validator type.
